title: Harp guitar

infobox.value: [321.322](/wiki/List_of_musical_instruments_by_Hornbostel%E2%80%93Sachs_number:_321.322 "List of musical instruments by Hornbostel–Sachs number: 321.322") ([plucked](/wiki/Plucked_string_instrument "Plucked string instrument"))

is a [guitar](/wiki/Guitar "Guitar")-based [stringed instrument](/wiki/Stringed_instrument "Stringed instrument") generally defined as a "[guitar](/wiki/Guitar "Guitar"), in any of its accepted forms, with any number of additional unstopped strings that can accommodate individual plucking."[[3]](#cite_note-3) The word "harp" is used in reference to its [harp](/wiki/Harp "Harp")-like unstopped open strings. A harp guitar must have at least one unfretted string lying off the main fretboard, typically played as an open string.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Guitar-based string instrument

Harp guitar

|  |  |

The **harp guitar** is a [guitar](/wiki/Guitar "Guitar")-based [stringed instrument](/wiki/Stringed_instrument "Stringed instrument") generally defined as a "[guitar](/wiki/Guitar "Guitar"), in any of its accepted forms, with any number of additional unstopped strings that can accommodate individual plucking."[[3]](#cite_note-3) The word "harp" is used in reference to its [harp](/wiki/Harp "Harp")-like unstopped open strings. A harp guitar must have at least one unfretted string lying off the main fretboard, typically played as an open string.

This family consists of many varieties of instrument configurations. Most readily identified are American harp guitars with either hollow arms, double necks or harp-like frames for supporting extra bass strings, and European bass guitars (or [contraguitars](/wiki/Contraguitar "Contraguitar")). Other harp guitars feature treble or mid-range floating strings, or various combinations of multiple floating string banks along with a standard guitar neck.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

## Electric harp guitars

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Harp_guitar&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Electric harp guitars")]

While most players of harp guitars play on acoustic instruments, a few of them also work with electric instruments. Notable artists who played electric harp guitars are Tim Donahue (who uses a fretless guitar section) and [Michael Hedges](/wiki/Michael_Hedges "Michael Hedges"). American musician [William Eaton](/wiki/William_Eaton_(guitarist) "William Eaton (guitarist)") both designs and plays electric harp guitars.[[5]](#cite_note-5) The Japanese [noise](/wiki/Noise_(music) "Noise (music)") band [Solmania](/wiki/Solmania "Solmania") built their own harp guitars.
[Yuri Landman](/wiki/Yuri_Landman "Yuri Landman") has built a 17 string electric harp guitar for [Finn Andrews](/wiki/Finn_Andrews "Finn Andrews") of [The Veils](/wiki/The_Veils "The Veils").[[6]](#cite_note-6) The instrument has an additional movable bridge on the harp section allowing players to pitch the harp section higher or lower.

## Players

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Harp_guitar&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Players")]

Historical harp guitar players include Polish guitarist-virtuoso [Marek Konrad Sokołowski](/w/index.php?title=Marek_Konrad_Soko%C5%82owski&action=edit&redlink=1 "Marek Konrad Sokołowski (page does not exist)") (1818-1884), the German composers and guitarists [Adam Darr](/wiki/Adam_Darr "Adam Darr") (1811–1866) and [Eduard Bayer](/wiki/Eduard_Bayer "Eduard Bayer") (1822–1908) and the Italian virtuosi Pasquale Taraffo (1887–1937),[[2]](#cite_note-players-2)[[7]](#cite_note-7) [Mario Maccaferri](/wiki/Mario_Maccaferri "Mario Maccaferri"), [Italo Meschi](/wiki/Italo_Meschi "Italo Meschi") (1887-1957) and Luigi Mozzani. Viennese and French virtuosos who often played instruments with extra, floating bass strings include [Carulli](/wiki/Ferdinando_Carulli "Ferdinando Carulli"), [Coste](/wiki/Napol%C3%A9on_Coste "Napoléon Coste"), [Giuliani](/wiki/Mauro_Giuliani "Mauro Giuliani"), [Mertz](/wiki/Johann_Kaspar_Mertz "Johann Kaspar Mertz"), Padovec and [Sor](/wiki/Fernando_Sor "Fernando Sor").[[8]](#cite_note-8)

English guitarist [John McLaughlin](/wiki/John_McLaughlin_(musician) "John McLaughlin (musician)") notably played a harp guitar, particularly with the group [Shakti](/wiki/Shakti_(band) "Shakti (band)"), often using the harp strings for [Indian](/wiki/Indian_classical_music "Indian classical music")-inspired drones and open chords. [Michael Hedges](/wiki/Michael_Hedges "Michael Hedges") was known for occasionally using a 1920s-era harp guitar, such as in his song "Because It's There".

[Andy McKee](/wiki/Andy_McKee "Andy McKee") also plays a harp guitar in a few of his songs, such as "Into The Ocean" and "The Friend I Never Met," the latter being a tribute to Hedges' "Because It's There." [Robbie Robertson](/wiki/Robbie_Robertson "Robbie Robertson") of [The Band](/wiki/The_Band_(band) "The Band (band)") used a harp guitar on the main title theme for their concert film *[The Last Waltz](/wiki/The_Last_Waltz "The Last Waltz")* and is seen playing the song on the instrument in the film's final scene. [Don Alder](/wiki/Don_Alder_(Acoustic_fingerstyle_guitarist) "Don Alder (Acoustic fingerstyle guitarist)") uses the Harp guitar in songs such as "Sayonara.calm" and "Man from Ladylane" a song dedicated to Stephen Bennett, founder of the Harp Guitar Gathering and one of the top current day harp guitar players. [Antoine Dufour](/wiki/Antoine_Dufour "Antoine Dufour") also uses the instrument occasionally, such as in his song "Paroxysm".

[Oleg Timofeyev](/wiki/Oleg_Timofeyev "Oleg Timofeyev") primarily uses a traditional Russian seven-string guitar with floating sub-bass strings.
American harp-guitarist Gregg Miner owns the world's largest collection of harp guitars ("The Miner Museum") and runs harpguitars.net, a website dedicated to education and promotion of harp guitars. Alongside Stephen Bennett, he has organized, attended and documented every Harp Guitar Gathering since its inception.

[Alfred Karnes](/wiki/Alfred_Karnes "Alfred Karnes") (1891–1958) was an [Old-time](/wiki/Old-time_music "Old-time music") Country and [Southern Gospel](/wiki/Southern_Gospel "Southern Gospel") singer and guitarist who recorded at the famous [Bristol Sessions](/wiki/Bristol_Sessions "Bristol Sessions") in 1927. He was known for songs such as "I'm Bound for the Promised Land" and "To the Work". His records are the only known use of the harp-guitar in Old Time Music.
